meanings.definition: Sick, weak, or not in good physical or mental health.
examples: She spent months caring for her ailing grandfather.
The ailing dog refused to eat anything.
Doctors treated several ailing patients during the outbreak.
His ailing health forced him to retire early.

meanings.definition: Causing trouble, pain, or illness to someone.
examples: Nobody could understand what was ailing the child.
Stress has been ailing him for years.
The mechanic tried to discover what ailed the old engine.
She asked gently what was ailing her friend.

meanings.definition: Very tired physically or mentally, usually after hard work, stress, or long effort.
examples: The weary travelers finally reached the village.
She looked wearier after working through the night.
His voice sounded weary and exhausted.
The workers walked home wearily after the long day.

meanings.definition: To make someone tired, bored, or exhausted.
examples: The endless arguments wearied everyone in the room.
Long journeys often weary young children.
She was wearied by constant complaints.
The repetitive work had been wearying the employees for months.
The unceasing rain flooded the streets.
She admired his unceasing determination.
Traffic noise continued in an unceasing stream outside.
The machine worked unceasingly throughout the night.

meanings.definition: Exposed to strong winds or shaped by constant wind.
examples: They walked across the windswept beach at sunset.
A lonely cottage stood on the windswept hill.
Her hair looked wild and windswept after the storm.
The windswept plains stretched endlessly into the distance.

meanings.definition: To extend, spread, or make something longer or wider.
examples: The road stretches across the desert for miles.
She stretched her arms after waking up.
Clouds stretched across the evening sky.
The fabric had stretched after being washed.

meanings.definition: To reach or continue over a distance, area, or period of time.
examples: The forest stretches from the mountains to the sea.
Their friendship stretched over many decades.
Long shadows were stretching across the field.
The meeting stretched late into the night.

meanings.definition: A continuous area, period, or extent of something.
examples: A long stretch of empty road lay ahead.
The river passes through a quiet stretch of forest.
He worked for hours without a single stretch of rest.
That difficult stretch of life taught her patience.

meanings.definition: An act of extending the body or limbs to relax muscles.
examples: She did a quick stretch before running.
After sitting all day, he enjoyed a long stretch.
The trainer taught the students simple stretches.
Morning stretches help reduce stiffness.

meanings.definition: A loud, unpleasant, and continuous noise.
examples: The din of traffic filled the busy street.
Children shouted above the din of the crowd.
The factory produced a constant din all day.
He struggled to hear over the din of the storm.

meanings.definition: To repeat something loudly and continuously until it becomes hard to ignore.
examples: Teachers dinned the rules into the students repeatedly.
The warning was dinned into everyone’s minds.
Parents often din good manners into children.
The coach kept dinning instructions throughout practice.

meanings.definition: Unable to be touched physically; existing as an idea, feeling, quality, or concept rather than a material object.
examples: Love and trust are intangible qualities.
The project created intangible benefits for the community.
Fear can be powerful even though it is intangible.
Artists often try to express intangible emotions through music.

meanings.definition: Difficult to clearly define, measure, or fully understand.
examples: There was an intangible sadness in his voice.
Success sometimes depends on intangible factors like confidence.
The film captured an intangible sense of loneliness.
Her influence on the team was subtle but intangible.
